59.reason: [Insertion does not maintain strictly ascending order in all cases, especially when inserting at front or end]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This line causes an error because if the loop exits without breaking (i.e., when e->adj is larger than all existing nodes), p points to the last node, and we correctly append e. However, if the new node should be inserted at the beginning (e->adj < head->adj), this logic fails to handle that case, leading to unsorted adjacency lists which violates the requirement to visit neighbors in ascending order.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This line always appends 'e' at the end regardless of order. If the loop breaks early due to insertion in middle, this overwrites the correct insertion and breaks the list. Also, if the new node should be inserted before head, it's not handled.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This line causes an error because if the loop exits without breaking (i.e., when e->adj is greater than all existing nodes), p points to the last node, and we correctly append e. However, if the new node should be inserted at the beginning (e->adj < head->adj), this logic fails to handle that case, leading to unsorted adjacency lists which violates the requirement to visit neighbors in ascending order.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Insertion does not maintain strictly ascending order in all cases, especially when inserting at front or end]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This always appends 'e' at the end even if it was already inserted in the loop, causing duplicate insertion or incorrect ordering. Also, if the new node should be inserted before the first node (i.e., e->adj < head->adj), this logic fails to handle that case.]

168.reason: [Loop condition prevents checking the last node in adjacency list for deletion]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop condition 'p->next != NULL' skips the last node in the adjacency list, so if the vertex to delete is the last neighbor, it won't be removed. This leads to incorrect graph structure after deletion.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (isolated vertex), this causes segmentation fault. Also, if the first node is the one to delete, the loop condition p->next != NULL skips it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop condition 'p->next != NULL' skips the last node in the adjacency list, so if the vertex to delete is the last neighbor, it won't be removed. This leads to incorrect graph representation after deletion.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition prevents checking the last node in adjacency list for deletion]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (no neighbors), this loop condition causes undefined behavior by dereferencing NULL. Also, if the first node in the adjacency list is the one to delete, the loop never checks it because it starts checking from p->next.]

170.reason: [This assignment is incorrect: 'p' is the current node in the loop, but if the head itself matches 'del', we should update g[i].link directly, not via 'p->next'. Also, 'p' may not even be initialized properly in this context.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This assignment is incorrect: 'p' is the current node in the loop, but if the head itself matches 'del', we should update g[i].link directly, not via 'p->next'. Also, 'p' may not even be initialized properly in this context.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This assignment uses 'p' which may not be the head; also logic is flawed because it's inside a loop that assumes p->next exists. Should handle head deletion outside loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This assignment is incorrect: 'p' is not necessarily the head here. When checking g[i].link->adj == del, we should directly update g[i].link, but 'p' may not point to the head. Also, this check is inside a loop that starts from head, making it logically inconsistent.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This line uses 'p' which may not be initialized properly if the loop doesn't run; also, logic is flawed because it's inside a loop that assumes p->next exists.]

196.reason: [Dereferencing g[i].link when it might be NULL after deletion or initially]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (which can happen if the vertex has no edges, though unlikely per problem constraints), this dereference causes undefined behavior. Also, setting adj=-1 is unnecessary; just set link to NULL.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (no edges), dereferencing it causes segmentation fault.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (which can happen if the vertex has no edges, though unlikely per problem constraints), this dereference causes undefined behavior. Also, setting adj=-1 is unnecessary since we set link=NULL next.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Dereferencing g[i].link when it might be NULL after deletion or initially]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [If g[i].link is NULL (which can happen if the vertex has no edges), this dereferences a NULL pointer, causing a segmentation fault.]
